lidar_qa_and_confidence.description: Assess LiDAR point-cloud quality and compute confidence metrics for terrain extraction readiness.

qa_mode.label: QA mode {strict, balanced, permissive, auto} (default auto). Strict flags more anomalies; permissive reduces false alarms.
qa_mode.tooltip: Use strict for engineering-grade QA, balanced for general mapping, and permissive for noisy legacy datasets.

block_size.label: QA grid cell size in XY units (default tool profile). Smaller cells capture local issues; larger cells smooth noise.
block_size.tooltip: Typical values are 2-10 m in dense urban data and 5-25 m in regional datasets.

max_building_size.label: Maximum expected building/object width in XY units. Helps separate terrain from large elevated structures.

slope_threshold.label: Minimum edge slope in degrees for off-terrain detection. Higher values are more conservative in steep terrain.

elev_threshold.label: Reference-surface elevation threshold in Z units. Controls when points are treated as elevated outliers.

high_confidence_threshold.label: Threshold for high-confidence fraction metric (default from profile). Higher values produce stricter confidence scoring.

adapt_by_slope.label: Enable slope-adaptive tolerance scaling [optional]
adapt_by_slope.tooltip: Recommended for mountainous terrain. Automatically relaxes or tightens tolerances based on local slope.

lidar_terrain_product_suite.description: Generate terrain-focused raster products from LiDAR with consistent QA and surface controls.

profile.label: QA profile {strict, balanced, permissive} (default balanced). Controls filtering aggressiveness for terrain extraction.
profile.tooltip: Strict removes more non-terrain points but risks losing small terrain features; permissive preserves detail but may retain clutter.

block_size.label: Grid cell size in map units for intermediate surfaces. Smaller values preserve detail but increase runtime and memory.

max_building_size.label: Maximum expected structure width in map units. Used to stabilize terrain filtering near built features.

slope_threshold.label: Minimum edge slope (degrees) for off-terrain removal. Increase in rough terrain to reduce false positives.

elev_threshold.label: Reference-surface elevation threshold in Z units. Governs sensitivity to elevated non-ground returns.

z_factor.label: Vertical scaling factor for slope/hillshade products (default 1.0). Use when vertical and horizontal units differ.

hillshade_azimuth.label: Hillshade sun azimuth in degrees (default 315). Adjust for preferred illumination direction.

individual_tree_segmentation.description: Segment individual tree crowns from LiDAR using adaptive bandwidth and vegetation filtering.

input.label: Input LiDAR path or typed LiDAR object.

only_use_veg.label: Use only vegetation classes [optional]
only_use_veg.tooltip: Enabled by default. Restricts segmentation to vegetation class codes to reduce non-tree detections.

veg_classes.label: Vegetation class codes (comma-delimited or array; default 3,4,5). Set to match your classification scheme.

min_height.label: Minimum point height for candidate trees in Z units (default 2.0). Increase to ignore shrubs/understory.

max_height.label: Maximum point height [optional]. Use to exclude towers/noise or constrain species-specific canopy heights.

bandwidth_min.label: Minimum horizontal bandwidth (default 1.0). Lower values allow finer crown separation.

bandwidth_max.label: Maximum horizontal bandwidth (default 6.0). Higher values merge broader canopies and reduce over-segmentation.

adaptive_bandwidth.label: Enable adaptive per-seed bandwidth [optional]
adaptive_bandwidth.tooltip: Recommended in mixed-age stands. Estimates local crown geometry and adjusts kernel width automatically.

lidar_change_and_disturbance_analysis.description: Compare baseline and monitoring LiDAR epochs to quantify elevation change and disturbance intensity.

baseline_tiles.label: Baseline LiDAR tiles (ordered list or directory). Represents pre-change condition.

monitor_tiles.label: Monitoring LiDAR tiles (ordered list or directory). Represents post-change condition.

resolution.label: Output raster resolution in map units. Smaller cells improve local detail but increase runtime/storage.

min_change_m.label: Absolute elevation change threshold in meters. Changes below this are ignored in disturbance accounting.

output_prefix.label: Output prefix for per-tile delta rasters and JSON summaries.
